title: Navigate Without GPS
summary: Find your way using landmarks, street signs, and common sense.
learnerPromise: Find your way using landmarks, street signs, and common sense.
whyItMatters: Phones die. Signals fail. Batteries run out. Knowing how to navigate without technology helps you stay self-reliant.
realLifeUses: Your phone dies while you are exploring a new city.
You are in a remote area with no cell signal.
You want to save battery while hiking or traveling.
You need to give someone else directions without a map.
youWillLearn: Use landmarks to orient yourself and remember routes.
Read street signs, building numbers, and directional markers.
Estimate direction using the sun and known reference points.
Ask locals for clear, useful directions.
miniChallenge: Walk or ride to a familiar place without looking at your phone.
steps: Before you leave, mentally note the major landmarks and street names along your route.
Start moving. Look up, not down — read street signs and building names.
Use the sun or a known landmark to confirm you are heading the right direction.
If lost, stop and observe. Find a major road or landmark to reorient yourself.
When needed, ask a local for directions using specific landmarks, not just street names.
completionCriteria: You reach a destination without using a GPS or map app.
You can reorient yourself after getting lost using environmental cues.
commonProblems: Everything looks the same — look for unique landmarks like towers, murals, or shops.
Street signs are missing or confusing — follow the traffic flow or head to a main road.
You are unsure about asking for help — locals usually know the fastest route.
tips: Note landmarks on the way out so you can retrace your steps.
Major roads lead to other major roads — when lost, find the biggest street.
The sun rises in the east and sets in the west — use it as a compass.
